title: Beijing 2-Day Heritage Tour
metaTitle: Beijing 2-Day Heritage Tour | Flights, Hotels & Tours Included
metaDescription: Book a 2-day trip to Beijing, China: round-trip flights, hotel, breakfast, transfers, and guided tours including Forbidden City and Great Wall at Mutianyu. Prices from $1306 per person (estimate).
highlights: Great Wall at Mutianyu or Badaling
overview: Spend 2 days discovering Beijing, China — Continental climate — hot summers (highs around 31-32°C) and very cold, dry winters (highs around 2°C, occasional snow). March-May and September-October, for mild temperatures and generally clearer skies ideal for visiting the Great Wall. This itinerary blends Forbidden City, Great Wall at Mutianyu, Tiananmen Square with free time to explore at your own pace, backed by round-trip flights, handpicked accommodation, and a dedicated travel consultant from enquiry through departure.
included: Round-trip economy-class airfare from your selected origin city (upgrade to premium economy or business available)
1 night accommodation in your selected category (Budget, Mid-range, Premium, or Luxury)
Daily local transport as per the itinerary
Guided tours including Forbidden City, Great Wall at Mutianyu, Tiananmen Square
All entrance fees for attractions and activities listed in the itinerary
Applicable taxes and OTA service fee
Dedicated travel consultant support from booking through departure
notIncluded: Visa fees and travel document costs (see Visa Information)
Travel insurance (strongly recommended, available as an add-on)
Lunches and dinners except where noted in the itinerary
Optional activities not listed in the day-by-day itinerary
Airline seat selection and baggage upgrades
Any fare difference for premium economy or business class upgrades

itinerary.title: Arrive in Beijing
itinerary.description: Land at Beijing Capital International Airport (PEK) and take your included private transfer to your hotel. With limited time on the ground, head straight out to Forbidden City — The vast former imperial palace complex at the heart of Beijing. Round off the evening with a welcome dinner introducing you to local flavors.

itinerary.title: Beijing Highlights & Departure
itinerary.description: Start early with a visit to Great Wall at Mutianyu — One of the best-preserved, less crowded sections of the Great Wall. Grab a last taste of local life before your private transfer to PEK for your onward flight.

travelTips: Set up a VPN before arrival for blocked Western apps and sites.
Book Forbidden City tickets online in advance — daily visitor numbers are capped.
Choose Mutianyu over Badaling for a less crowded, equally spectacular Great Wall section.
Check the air quality index before planning an outdoor-heavy day, especially in winter.
With a short stay, book your must-see attractions in advance to avoid losing time to queues.
packingList: Copies of booking confirmations and travel insurance
Lightweight, breathable clothing and a reusable water bottle
Insulated jacket and warm layers for cold weather
A VPN set up before arrival
Warm layers for cold, dry winters; breathable clothing for hot summers
Comfortable shoes for extensive walking at the Forbidden City and Great Wall
A face mask for occasional high-pollution days
thingsToDo: Hiking a less-restored Great Wall section (Jiankou, for experienced hikers)
Toboggan ride down from the Mutianyu wall section
thingsToAvoid: Arriving without a VPN arranged in advance.
Visiting the Great Wall at Badaling on a weekend or holiday — it gets extremely crowded.
Underestimating the Forbidden City's scale — budget at least half a day.
topRestaurants: Da Dong Roast Duck — Peking duck ($$$)
hiddenGems: Jingshan Park hilltop view over the Forbidden City
adventureActivities: Hiking a less-restored Great Wall section (Jiankou, for experienced hikers)
Toboggan ride down from the Mutianyu wall section
luxuryExperiences: Private Great Wall tour with a historian guide
Peking duck tasting menu at Da Dong
Suite stay near the Forbidden City
honeymoonExperiences: Sunset at the Temple of Heaven
Rickshaw tour of the hutongs at dusk
Private Great Wall visit avoiding crowds
budgetExperiences: Jingshan Park (low-cost entry) for a Forbidden City viewpoint
localEtiquette: A VPN arranged before arrival is essential — Google, WhatsApp, and Instagram are blocked.
Business cards are exchanged with both hands and a slight bow.
Most payments are cashless via WeChat Pay or Alipay; carry cash as backup.
localTransportGuide: The Beijing Subway is extensive, cheap, and has English signage, making it the easiest way to reach most major sites. For the Great Wall, most visitors book a tour bus or private driver, since public transport options are limited and time-consuming.
bestTimeToVisit: March-May and September-October, for mild temperatures and generally clearer skies ideal for visiting the Great Wall.
weather: Continental climate — hot summers (highs around 31-32°C) and very cold, dry winters (highs around 2°C, occasional snow).
visaInformation: Most nationalities including the US, UK, EU, Canada, Australia, and India need a Chinese visa arranged in advance, though many travelers qualify for a 144-hour visa-free transit if arriving/departing via certain international gateways.
averageFlightTime: ~10.2h from major hubs (estimate; varies by origin — see Suggested Flights)
cancellationPolicy: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.
termsAndConditions: Pricing shown is per person based on the traveler type and tier selected and is an estimate pending live fare and rate confirmation.
Passports must be valid for at least 6 months beyond the return date for most destinations — confirm the exact requirement for your nationality.
Itinerary order may change due to weather, local conditions, or attraction closures; inclusions remain equivalent.
Solo travelers incur a single-room supplement, reflected in the Solo pricing tier.
This package does not include international travel insurance unless explicitly listed under Included.
Prices shown in a currency other than USD are approximate conversions at the rate noted and are not a payment quote.

faqs.question: Do I need a visa to visit Beijing?
faqs.answer: Most nationalities including the US, UK, EU, Canada, Australia, and India need a Chinese visa arranged in advance, though many travelers qualify for a 144-hour visa-free transit if arriving/departing via certain international gateways.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit Beijing?
faqs.answer: March-May and September-October, for mild temperatures and generally clearer skies ideal for visiting the Great Wall. Continental climate — hot summers (highs around 31-32°C) and very cold, dry winters (highs around 2°C, occasional snow).

faqs.question: What's included in this package?
faqs.answer: Round-trip airfare, accommodation, daily breakfast, airport transfers, daily local transport, and guided tours to the attractions listed in the itinerary. See the Included / Not Included sections for full detail.

faqs.question: Can the itinerary be customized?
faqs.answer: Yes — this itinerary is a starting point. Your travel consultant can add, remove, or reorder activities, extend your stay, or upgrade your hotel category before you book.

faqs.question: What is the cancellation policy?
faqs.answer: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.

faqs.question: Is Beijing safe for tourists?
faqs.answer: Beijing is rated very safe for tourists, with low rates of violent crime. Usual travel precautions (watch belongings in crowds, use licensed taxis) still apply.

faqs.question: What currency should I bring to Beijing?
faqs.answer: The local currency is CNY. Cards and mobile payments are widely accepted, and ATMs are easy to find.

faqs.question: Is this package good for a honeymoon or family trip?
faqs.answer: This itinerary suits couples, solo travelers, and small groups equally well — talk to your consultant about tailoring it further.

reviews.author: Olivia Smith
reviews.title: Best vacation we've had
reviews.body: Our trip to Beijing was fantastic from start to finish. The hotel was clean and well located, and the guided visit to Temple of Heaven was a real highlight. Airport transfers were on time both ways.

reviews.author: Arjun Ivanov
reviews.title: A little rushed but still wonderful
reviews.body: Booked this as a couple trip and it was well paced. Tiananmen Square in particular stood out. A couple of the transfers ran a little late but nothing that spoiled the trip.

reviews.author: Hiroshi Dubois
reviews.title: Would book again in a heartbeat
reviews.body: Summer Palace alone was worth the trip, but combined with Forbidden City this itinerary really delivered. Breakfast at the hotel was good every day and the local guide was knowledgeable.

reviews.author: Grace Johnson
reviews.title: Consultant made it effortless
reviews.body: We added a couple of optional activities beyond 798 Art District and don't regret it. Good value for what was included, and the food recommendations from our guide were spot on.

reviews.author: Chen Kowalski
reviews.title: Loved every moment
reviews.body: Booked this as a solo trip and it was well paced. Summer Palace in particular stood out. A couple of the transfers ran a little late but nothing that spoiled the trip.

reviews.author: Mia Müller
reviews.title: Great for families with kids
reviews.body: Everything was handled for us — flights, hotel, transfers, and tours to Nanluoguxiang hutong and Nanluoguxiang hutong. Great communication from the consultant before departure too.
